title: Law No. 60 of 2014 on Information Technology Crimes
summary: Not generative-AI-specific, but directly relevant where generative AI is used to commit existing information technology crimes such as fraudulent acquisition, data falsification, misuse of tools, unauthorised access, identity-related misuse, or other digital offences.

theme_notes.ai_emerging_tech.generative_ai: Baseline cybercrime law relevant to AI-enabled deception, fraud, falsification, and misuse.
theme_notes.online_content.online_media_licensing: Cybercrime law containing online content offences.
theme_notes.online_content.content_standards_moderat: Online content criminal offences under the cybercrime law.
theme_notes.online_content.gaming_esports: Cybercrime law relevant to online gaming and esports platforms.

notes: Related instruments: Proposed 2025 AI-generated deepfakes and fabricated videos amendment Issuing authority unverified in the source; assigned to the Government of Bahrain pending confirmation.
